items.title: Sensex today | Stock Markets Live Updates: Sensex gains 300 pts, Nifty holds above 24,500; Tech Mahindra, Infy lead gainers
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE: Sensex climbed 209.85 pts or 0.27% to 78,494.92; and Nifty 50 was up 60.50 pts or 0.25% to 24,490.85.

items.title: India fashion retailer Trent tumbles 11% as revenue growth disappoints
items.summary: Shares of one of India's biggest fashion retailers, Trent, fell over 10% after the company reported weak earnings growth.

items.title: MRPL first state refiner to charter Iraqi crude cargo since Hormuz blockade
items.summary: MRPL has booked the Aframax tanker Jasmin Joy to load ​crude from Iraq's ‌Basrah oil terminal on July 19-20, the sources said.

items.title: IIFL initiates coverage on Adani Power with target price at Rs 240, estimates EBITDA to quadruple by FY33-35E
items.summary: IIFL initiated coverage on Adani Power with a 'Buy' rating and Rs 240 target price. The brokerage forecasts EBITDA to quadruple by FY33-35E due to growth pipeline. Adani Power is building significant new coal capacity, expanding its operational base. Free cash flow from operations is expected to rise substantially after portfolio buildout. The company also plans future ventures into nuclear and hydro power generation.

items.title: IT giants delay fresher hiring as business uncertainty clouds campus offers
items.summary: India's largest IT recruiters are delaying onboarding and, in some cases, withdrawing offers made to engineering graduates, reflecting cautious hiring amid an uncertain business environment and adding to concerns over slowing entry-level technology jobs.

items.title: Sensex at 1 lakh in one year? Morgan Stanley sees 25% chance; Here’s what could trigger the next stock market rally
items.summary: Morgan Stanley's India equity strategist Ridham Desai outlined three possible outcomes for the market over the coming year, assigning a 25% probability to its bull case, a 50% probability to its base case, and a 25% probability to its bear case.

items.title: ​Microsoft stocks in focus: What the latest layoffs mean for investors​
items.summary: Microsoft is cutting nearly 4,800 jobs as its new fiscal year begins. These layoffs primarily impact the commercial sales and Xbox gaming divisions. The company states this restructuring reflects industry evolution and changing business priorities.

items.title: E-bike injury payouts top £110m and push up insurance premiums
items.summary: It is only seven years since the first claim over an injury from a micromobility vehicle was made.

items.title: IIFL initiates coverage on Adani Power shares. Should you buy?
items.summary: On Tuesday, Adani Power share price fell over 1.30% to ₹217 apiece on NSE.

items.title: Trent shares crash over 11% after Q1FY27 business update. Here's what spooked investors
items.summary: Trent share price looked set to snap its six-day winning streak, as the retail stock crashed more than 11% in intraday trade on the BSE on Tuesday, 7 July.

items.title: India bond rally pauses ahead of state debt sale
items.summary: Indian government bonds traded range-bound early Tuesday, pausing a three-session rally. Traders awaited a large state-debt auction, which will sell bonds worth 213.50 billion rupees. Oil and U.S. Treasury yields trading sideways provided no fresh directional triggers for the market. Sustained foreign buying is expected to continue underpinning sentiment in the bond market. Monsoon-led inflation concerns have also eased as rainfall improved across the country.

items.title: Hanwha Ocean shares sink 23% as it loses bid to build Canada's next fleet of submarines
items.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Monday that Germany's Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems would be the preferred supplier for delivering the submarines.

items.title: What could shape Samsung's growth in the coming quarters?
items.summary: Samsung Electronics has projected a record-breaking second quarter, with operating profit expected to surge 19-fold on strong demand for AI-related memory chips. While the outlook for its semiconductor business remains robust, investors are watching whether the current AI-driven spending cycle can sustain Samsung's growth momentum.Samsung Electronics has projected a record-breaking second quarter, with operating profit expected to surge 19-fold on strong demand for AI-related memory chips. While the outlook for its semiconductor business remains robust, investors are watching whether the curre

items.title: Recently listed Waterways Leisure Tourism shares rise nearly 6% after announcement of stock split move
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism's share price rose nearly 6% on July 7 following the announcement of a board meeting on July 10 to discuss a stock split of its ₹10 shares, aiming to enhance liquidity while keeping shareholder value unchanged.

items.title: RITES shares soar 9% after bagging a $36 million locomotive supply order from South Africa
items.summary: RITES shares rallied after securing a $36 million locomotive supply contract. The company also received a Rs 175.41 crore consultancy project from BBAU. These new orders will be added to RITES' growing order book. Execution of these contracts is scheduled over the next 20 to 30 months. RITES recently signed an MoU with CONCOR for terminal development services.

items.title: E20 petrol may affect turbocharged engines, damage rubber fuel-system parts in E10 vehicles; no issues found in two-wheelers: ARAI report
items.summary: India has completed its nationwide E20 petrol rollout, which raises concerns for older vehicles. ARAI tests showed mixed results for four-wheeler engines using E20 fuel. Two-wheeler durability tests indicated acceptable performance with E20 fuel. Automakers state E20 is safe for existing vehicles after extensive testing. Higher ethanol blends like E25 could present greater challenges for the automotive industry.

items.title: Samsung Electronics shares fall as capex, demand concerns cloud record Q2 profit report
items.summary: Samsung Electronics shares fell even after record preliminary Q2 profit as capex and demand concerns clouded the outlook.

items.title: SpaceX set to join Nasdaq-100 today. Will index entry fuel its next rally?
items.summary: SpaceX is set to join the Nasdaq-100 index today, July 7. This inclusion could trigger approximately $4.3 billion in passive investment inflows. Companies joining the index often experience a share price boost from tracking funds. SpaceX was also recently added to the Russell 1000 index. Investors will observe if the index entry fuels further stock gains.

items.title: This smallcap aerospace stock could soar 91%, predicts Nuvama
items.summary: Nuvama has initiated coverage on Aequs with a 'Buy' rating and a target price of Rs 444, implying a 91% upside. The brokerage expects strong earnings growth, backed by the company's $889 million order book, vertically integrated aerospace manufacturing capabilities, and long-term contracts with global aerospace OEMs.

items.title: Apollo Hospitals share price turns negative after hitting record high of ₹8,948.10 apiece on BSE. Do you own?
items.summary: Apollo Hospitals stock hits record high following gains in DalalStreet. It has added 2% in the last 1 week, 6.5% in 1 month, 21% in 3 months, and 19% in 6 months. Meanwhile, it has given multibagger returns in thel ong term, soaring 138% in 5 years.

items.title: China gives its investors more access to bonds in Hong Kong
items.summary: Authorities raise quota for offshore ‘connect’ scheme in bid to boost international use of renminbi

items.title: FIIs return, crude eases: Markets open higher with Nifty at 24,453
items.summary: The Sensex opened at 78,461.16 against its previous close of 78,285.07

items.title: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with U.S. companies as OpenAI, Anthropic costs surge
items.summary: Recent model releases from Chinese companies including DeepSeek and Z.ai are seen by many as highly competitive compared to leading U.S. frontier systems.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs over $63,000 as buying interest returns; key on-chain metric signals market stabilization
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed past $63,000 as buying interest returned on Tuesday. Key on-chain metrics indicated market stabilization after recent fluctuations. Ethereum also saw gains, supported by stronger network activity and stablecoin supply. Major altcoins experienced mixed movements, with some falling and others rising. Analysts suggest further confirmation is needed for a decisive trend reversal.

items.title: Global LNG trade hit record in 2025, but Middle East conflict clouds outlook, IGU says
items.summary: Global LNG trade rose 6.3% ‌to ⁠436.98 million metric tons in 2025, the fastest ⁠rate of growth since 2022

items.title: Teja Engineering Industries shares hit the upper circuit after a stellar 90% listing gain
items.summary: Teja Engineering Industries' share price opened at ₹418 on the NSE, a premium of 90% over the issue price of ₹220. The SME stock extended gains further to hit its upper price band of ₹438.90.

items.title: Titan hits 52-week high as Q1 numbers spark early rally
items.summary: Titan’s watches and eyecare segments both clocked 23% growth in Q1 FY27, beating estimates. International business posted 128% growth

items.title: Global Market: UK regulator urged to consider rules for AI chatbots in financial advice
items.summary: A UK regulatory review has recommended that the Financial Conduct Authority consider bringing AI models such as ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini within its regulatory framework as their influence on consumer financial decisions grows. The report also warned of systemic risks from increasing reliance on a handful of AI and cloud providers.

items.title: Power Grid Corporation secures 80 billion yen loan from JBIC for Khavda-Nagpur HVDC project
items.summary: Power Grid Corporation share price has fallen 3% in three months, and has gained 8% in six months. The PSU stock has fallen 16% in two years, while it has jumped 49% in three years.

items.title: Upcoming IPO: Cult.fit files draft papers with SEBI to raise ₹950 crore via IPO
items.summary: Cult.fit has filed a draft red herring prospectus with SEBI for an IPO, featuring ₹950 crore in fresh equity and an offer-for-sale of 17.86 crore shares. Funds from the fresh issue aim to cover fitness centre expenses, repay borrowings, and support marketing initiatives.

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers stock tumbles 9% despite strong Q1FY27 business update
items.summary: The stock declined to ₹348.35 on the NSE from the previous close of ₹381.25.

items.title: Vedanta Iron and Steel, Vedanta Aluminium, 2 other stocks drop up to 5%. What’s spooking investors?
items.summary: Recently listed Vedanta stocks experienced a significant decline on Tuesday morning. Vedanta Iron and Steel shares surged sharply after a substantial investment by Premji Invest. This smallcap counter saw its market capitalization more than double before the rally lost momentum. Vedanta Aluminium shares dropped despite reporting record quarterly production figures. Vedanta Oil & Gas and Vedanta Power also saw their stock prices fall since listing.

items.title: Crude oil futures gain on reports of attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations said a tanker has reported being hit by an unknown projectile on the port side causing a fire, whilst travelling southbound

items.title: Titan share price hits new high; global brokerages give 'buy' tag to Rekha Jhunjhunwala portfolio stock post Q1 update
items.summary: Titan reported a 41% YoY increase in consumer business growth for Q1 FY27, driven by strong demand across various segments. Brokerages remain positive, with CLSA and HSBC reiterating 'Buy' ratings and raising EPS estimates amid the company's expanding retail presence.

items.title: India’s 163.65 LT fertilizer stock, rising production to help meet Kharif demand, says govt
items.summary: Natural gas supply to urea plants, which had dropped to nearly 65% during the disruption after war has now been fully restored to 100%, allowing all plants to operate at full capacity, officials said.

items.title: Vedanta Aluminium wipes off Rs 25,000 cr worth investor wealth in under a month. Time to buy?
items.summary: Vedanta Aluminium shares have dropped significantly since their listing date. This decline has erased substantial investor wealth in a short period. However, analysts maintain a positive outlook on the company's future. Structural demand for aluminium is expected to remain strong globally. The company's cost reduction initiatives are poised for future growth.

items.title: Cult.fit files for IPO to raise up to ₹950 crore
items.summary: The OFS component, by a clutch of existing shareholders, can potentially take Cult.fit's IPO size to up to ₹4,000 crore

items.title: Cochin Shipyard shares drop 4% as Government OFS opens at floor price of ₹1,400
items.summary: The OFS has a base offer size of 2.52% of the company’s paid-up equity capital, or 66.29 lakh shares, with an equal oversubscription option taking the total potential dilution to 5.04%

items.title: Broadcasters, streamers step up investment in premium non-fiction content
items.summary: Indian broadcasters and streaming platforms are significantly increasing investment in premium non-fiction content. Unscripted programming now accounts for nearly thirty percent of all content across platforms. Companies are betting on reality and comedy formats to attract viewers and build franchises. Netflix and Prime Video are expanding their unscripted content with homegrown and international shows. Broadcasters like Star Plus and Sony are also strengthening their non-fiction offerings to boost viewership.

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers shares tumble 7% despite strong Q1 update. Details here
items.summary: Kalyan Jewellers reported approximately 38% revenue growth in Q1 FY2027. This growth was driven by strong domestic and international business performance. The company's gold recirculation campaign also showed encouraging progress. However, the stock declined as investors booked profits after the update.

items.title: Seemax Resources share price lists at ₹112.80 on BSE SME, a 20% discount to SME IPO price
items.summary: Seemax Resources debuted on BSE SME at ₹112.80, 20% below its IPO price of ₹141. The IPO was subscribed 3.60 times, with significant interest from QIBs at 17.19 times. Seemax focuses on material handling solutions across diverse industries.

items.title: Rs 18,000 crore crash in Trent shares explained, and should you buy the dip
items.summary: Trent shares fell sharply on Tuesday, erasing nearly Rs 18,000 crore in market value, after its Q1 revenue growth of 19% missed Street estimates. While weak store productivity and slowing expansion raised concerns, most brokerages remain bullish, calling the slowdown seasonal, though Citi warns of structural challenges and intensifying competition.

items.title: Atharva Polyplast share price lists with 15% premium at ₹69 on BSE
items.summary: Atharva Polyplast share price opened at ₹69 apiece on BSE SME. The stock listed at 15% premium over the IPO price of ₹60.

items.title: Rupee rises 15 paise to 95.28 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: The Indian rupee gained fifteen paise against the US dollar in early trade. Saudi Arabia's significant slash in August crude oil prices for Asia improved market sentiment. Lower Middle East risk premiums and improved supply conditions contributed to the rupee's appreciation. Meanwhile, domestic equity markets saw an upward trend with the Sensex advancing. Foreign institutional investors purchased equities worth Rs 243.03 crore on Monday.

items.title: Nvidia server delay report sends Asian tech stocks sliding
items.summary: The stock declines showed investors are wary of any potential negative developments after big run-ups in AI-related stocks

items.title: Indian stocks to open higher on positive global cues
items.summary: Ponmudi R, CEO of Enrich Money, said Indian equities are expected to trade with a constructive bias, supported by favourable global cues, resilient domestic fundamentals and improving investor sentiment

items.title: Stocks surge on chip news, oil holds at pre-war levels
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average ended the day up 0.29%, while the S&P 500 jumped 0.72% and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 1.12%

items.title: Stocks to watch, July 7: Cochin Shipyard, Tanfac, Central Mine, Mamata Machinery
items.summary: Here are the latest updated for Cochin Shipyard, Tanfac, Central Mine, Mamata Machinery, Zen Tech, Mobikwik, PowerGrid, Varun Beverages, Blue Jet

items.title: Kalyan Jewellers share price crashes 9% after Q1FY27 business update; details here
items.summary: Kalyan Jewellers share price crashed almost 9% in morning trade on the BSE on Tuesday, 7 July, after the company announced its Q1FY27 business updates, reporting a consolidated revenue growth of approximately 38% for the quarter.

items.title: India's fuel consumption drops 3.7% in June from previous month
items.summary: India's fuel consumption fell approximately 3.7% in June from the previous month. Liquefied petroleum gas consumption saw a significant year-on-year decrease of over fourteen percent. Gasoline sales were down month-on-month but showed annual growth, while diesel consumption rose annually. Naphtha sales experienced a substantial year-on-year drop, and fuel oil usage also declined. Windfall taxes on certain fuel exports were adjusted by the government last month.

items.title: HSBC pulls back from risky credit after bankruptcy scare, FT reports
items.summary: HSBC is reportedly scaling back its involvement in riskier private credit deals, according to the Financial Times. This strategic shift indicates a move towards a more cautious approach in the private credit market, signaling a potential change in the bank's lending appetite for more speculative investments.

items.title: Sumitomo Chemical among 4 stocks that closed crossing below VWAP
items.summary: Four Nifty500 stocks, Zydus Wellness, Sumitomo Chemical India, Max Healthcare Institute, and Zensar Technologies, closed over 1.5% below their VWAP on July 6, indicating potential bearish momentum and increased selling pressure, according to technical scan data.

items.title: UAE’s Adnoc strikes $1bn deal for Shell’s South African fuels business
items.summary: Acquistion of 580 service stations and other assets marks latest overseas expansion by Adnoc subsidiary

items.title: US Stock Market | Hedge funds extend selloff in tech hardware stocks ahead of earnings: Goldman Sachs
items.summary: US hedge funds cut exposure to technology hardware stocks for a fourth straight week ahead of the earnings season, amid weakness in semiconductor shares and concerns over AI-related spending. They also trimmed holdings in industrials and consumer discretionary stocks while increasing allocations to index funds, ETFs and defensive sectors.

items.title: Mild rally in India bonds may persist as broader sentiment stays bullish
items.summary: The 10-year bond yield eased almost ‌3 basis points on Monday and has declined ‌by ⁠an aggregate of 37 bps over the ⁠past six weeks

items.title: RITES share price jumps over 8% after bagging overseas locomotive supply order worth $36 million
items.summary: RITES said it received an acceptance of its offer from Volantis Asset Finance (Pty) Ltd., South Africa, for the supply and commissioning of 4000 HP Cape Gauge diesel electric locomotives.

items.title: Poonawalla Fincorp among 4 stocks closed above VWAP, signalling a bullish shift
items.summary: On July sixth, four Nifty500 stocks saw closing prices rally by over 1.5 percent. This movement above their Volume Weighted Average Price suggests a potential bullish trend. Karur Vysya Bank and Poonawalla Fincorp showed significant price increases. Manappuram Finance and Swan Corp also experienced positive trading activity.

items.title: Global market: Hong Kong launches gold clearing system, revives futures trading to strengthen bullion hub ambitions
items.summary: Hong Kong launched a central clearing system and revived gold futures trading. These initiatives aim to strengthen the city's gold trading infrastructure. Tax incentives will also support gold trading and settlement activities. A "Delivery Connect" scheme facilitates cross-border gold settlements with Shanghai. Hong Kong seeks to become Asia's leading gold trading center.

items.title: Global Market: China emerges as diversification bet for investors amid market volatility
items.summary: Global investors now see Chinese assets as diversification, not just growth. China's markets show resilience amid global volatility and geopolitical tensions. This shift encourages fresh inflows into Chinese bond and equity markets. The yuan's strength and policy stability drive investor interest in China. Despite concerns, China's independent market dynamics enhance its portfolio appeal.

items.title: RIL expands CBM production contract worth Rs 163 crore with SWPE
items.summary: Reliance Industries Limited has extended its Coal Bed Methane production contract with South West Pinnacle Exploration. This awarded work is expected to generate revenue from the second quarter of FY 2026-27. South West Pinnacle Exploration also received a separate contract from CMPDI for exploration services. This pact valued at Rs 5.89 crore will strengthen SWPE's order book. The exploration work is scheduled for completion within 180 days.

items.title: Growing UK pension surpluses fuel debate over what to do with excess funds
items.summary: Corporate defined benefit schemes have seen an improvement in funding levels since 2019 thanks to rising gilt yields

items.title: Roadside rescuers may still bump up against a driverless future
items.summary: Self-driving cabs crash less, which is bad news for the AA’s and RAC’s accident assistance businesses

items.title: HSBC pulls back from riskier private credit lending
items.summary: Europe’s biggest bank told some clients in recent weeks that it would not renew facilities

items.title: Greek shipping companies made almost $4bn carrying Russian oil in past three years
items.summary: Dynacom Tankers, Stealth Maritime and Onassis Group boost revenues from transporting Russian fuel under G7 price cap regime

items.title: How to manage the next emerging markets capital flows boom
items.summary: Foreign exchange reserves are not the only way to limit the build-up of speculative, short-term liabilities

items.title: The Big Four’s problems Down Under
items.summary: Elite consulting firms accused of data breaches by Australian government

items.title: India's favourite safe haven? Why gold hype is facing a reality check in 2026
items.summary: Gold's appeal as a safe haven has slowed after a strong rally this year. Profit-booking and easing geopolitical tensions have led to a market reassessment. The US Federal Reserve's interest rate stance remains a significant factor for gold prices. Investors are currently prioritizing growth assets like equities over protection trades.

items.title: Sensex rises over 150 points, Nifty above 24,450 as market extends gains for the 5th consecutive day
items.summary: Indian stock markets traded higher on Tuesday, extending gains for a fifth consecutive session. Oil prices remained near pre-war levels, and foreign investors continued their buying trend. Sensex and Nifty both saw upward movement, with broader markets also participating in the rally. Certain sectors like IT advanced, while others experienced declines. Experts anticipate an uptrend supported by economic fundamentals and favorable interest rates.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei falls as Samsung-led chip sell-off weighs on regional tech stocks
items.summary: Samsung Electronics fell more than 5% after its forecast for a 19-fold jump in second-quarter operating profit from a year earlier disappointed investors who had expected a stronger earnings outlook

items.title: Gold pulls back from two-week high on stronger dollar; Fed minutes on tap
items.summary: The dollar gained 0.1%, making ​the yellow metal more expensive ‌for overseas buyers

items.title: Kotak-backed Biorad Medisys in talks to raise ₹400 crore ahead of a potential IPO
items.summary: Medical devices maker Biorad Medisys is raising about ₹400 crore in a pre-IPO round at a ₹10,000 crore valuation as it prepares for a potential ₹1,500 crore public listing.

items.title: Gold prices fall Rs 1,000/10 gram; silver down Rs 3,000/kg ahead of US Fed minutes. Key levels to track
items.summary: Gold and silver prices opened lower on the MCX Tuesday, extending recent losses. Investors await U.S. Federal Reserve minutes for monetary policy clues. MCX silver futures for September 2026 delivery fell and gold futures for August 2026 delivery also declined. Spot gold and silver prices also saw decreases in international markets. Experts suggest buying gold and silver on dips with strict stop-loss adherence.

items.title: Cochin Shipyard share price falls 4% as OFS opens today for non-retail investors. Details here
items.summary: Cochin Shipyard stock opened at ₹1,451.20 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹1,524.10 apiece on Monday.

items.title: Reliance Industries shares in focus as company gets Sebi's warning over alleged insider trading
items.summary: Reliance Industries received a warning letter from market regulator Sebi. The letter cited alleged insider trading by two employees and a relative. Sebi investigated trades made while possessing unpublished price-sensitive information. The company stated it will address the concerns raised by the regulator. Sebi's warning is cautionary and imposes no financial restrictions.

items.title: Kusumgar's Rs 650-crore IPO opens tomorrow; GMP signals nearly 40% listing gain
items.summary: Kusumgar's Rs 650-crore IPO will open for subscription on July 8, with the issue commanding a grey market premium of around Rs 166. The offer is entirely an offer for sale, while the company has reported steady revenue and profit growth over the past two financial years.

items.title: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems declares ₹951 crore fund raise via preferential shares
items.summary: Apollo Micro Systems has approved a capital-raising plan exceeding ₹3,322 crore through equity shares and warrants. An Extraordinary General Meeting is set for August 4 to seek shareholder approval for increasing authorized capital and issuing shares to investors.

items.title: Dixon Tech, Coforge, 8 other stocks among Motilal’s non-Nifty ideas ahead of Q1 results
items.summary: Motilal Oswal has identified ten non-Nifty stocks for investors ahead of quarterly earnings. The brokerage anticipates healthy revenue growth across various market capitalizations. TVS Motor and Dixon Technologies are among the top picks with significant upside potential. Radico Khaitan and Indian Hotels Company also show promising revenue growth prospects. Coforge and Kirloskar Oil Engines are also highlighted for their future performance.

items.title: Atharva Polyplast, Seemax Resources among 6 SME IPOs set to debut on bourses today. Check details
items.summary: Six SME IPOs are set to debut on the stock exchanges today, with Atharva Polyplast, Seemax Resources, Sampark India Logistics and Kratikal Tech listing on the BSE SME platform, while Teja Engineering Industries and Vinit Mobile will debut on the NSE SME platform. Kratikal Tech topped the pack, raising Rs 39.69 crore and garnering a 205-times subscription.

items.title: Gold, silver rates decline on MCX ahead of US Fed's June meeting minutes; experts highlight key levels to watch
items.summary: MCX gold August contracts dropped 0.52% to ₹1,46,148 per 10 grams, while MCX silver September futures declined 1.14% down at ₹2,33,416 per kg.

items.title: Rupee opens 7 paise higher at 95.33 against US dollar
items.summary: The Indian rupee opened higher at 95.33 against the US dollar amid gains in Asian currencies, but remains weak after recent declines. Persistent dollar demand and expectations of US interest rate hikes pressure the rupee, despite rebound support from lower crude prices and RBI measures.

items.title: Founder of Brookfield-backed Avaada seeks $750 million loan
items.summary: Avaada Group founder Vineet Mittal plans to borrow $750 million from global lenders. This new financing will refinance the company's existing $1 billion credit facility. Several international banks are in talks to underwrite this significant debt deal. Avaada Group is also considering an initial public offering for its solar cell unit.

items.title: IT stocks are beginning to offer bank FD-like dividend yields. But is that a trap for investors?
items.summary: Indian IT stocks now offer dividend yields comparable to bank fixed deposits. These yields have risen because stock prices have significantly declined from their peaks. Companies possess substantial liquidity and are actively using buybacks and dividends for support. However, sector headwinds from AI and geopolitics create uncertainty for future growth. Investors face a polarized market with potential risks and rewards.

items.title: Where is the Sensex and Nifty headed?
items.summary: When the stock market peaked in September 2024, no one could have predicted the losses that investors would suffer in 2025 and 2026. But that is the nature of financial markets. No one can predict the future

items.title: Kospi plunges 8% as AI investors fear rally is overblown; trading halted for 20 mins
items.summary: South Korea's Kospi index experienced a significant 8% drop, led by chip stocks. Investors questioned the sustainability of record earnings driven by artificial intelligence demand. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, major index components, saw substantial declines. Other sectors like battery and shipbuilding also faced considerable losses. Foreign investors continued to be net sellers of South Korean equities.

items.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar
items.summary: The firm's shares fell sharply on Tuesday as some investors had expected an even stronger performance.

items.title: Stocks to buy for short term: Tata Steel, Raymond Realty among 5 stocks experts recommend for next 1-2 weeks
items.summary: Discover which stocks analysts are banking on for quick gains. From Tata Steel's rebound to Raymond Realty's breakout, this article highlights the top five stock picks that are attracting bullish sentiment in the market.

items.title: Cochin Shipyard shares fall 4% as OFS worth Rs 1,856 crore opens today at 7% discount
items.summary: Cochin Shipyard shares dropped as the government plans a significant stake sale. The offer for sale includes a base offer and an oversubscription option. This disinvestment move is part of the government's FY27 program. The offer opens for non-retail investors today and retail investors tomorrow. The government aims to raise over Rs 1,856 crore from this share sale.

items.title: Titan shares gain 2% on Q1 business update; consumer business grows 41% YoY
items.summary: Titan Company share price rose 2% after it saw a 41% year-on-year rise in the June quarter. Robust jewelry demand and an expanding retail network fueled this significant growth. The company added seventy-seven stores, increasing its total retail presence to 3,680 locations. Watches and EyeCare segments also reported strong twenty-three percent growth during the period.

items.title: US stock market to Kospi: Here’s world equity heatmap you should know before the opening of Indian stock market today
items.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,537 level, a premium of nearly 54 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a positive start for the Indian stock market indices.

items.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests Gail, Adani Ports shares to buy in near-term
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty 50, are poised to open slightly higher, driven by positive US market trends. The domestic market is experiencing a bullish trend, supported by strong foreign investments and improved investor sentiment.

items.title: World Cup in photos: Belgium beats U.S. 4-1 after Balogun controversy, Ronaldo exits
items.summary: The U.S. is out of the World Cup, losing 4-1 to Belgium in the Round of 16 in a match blemished by suspension of Folarin Balogun's red card. Ronaldo exits.

items.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints positive start; eight day trading stocks to buy on Tuesday, 7 July
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices are expected to open positively on Tuesday, following gains in the US market. The Sensex rose 521.16 points, while the Nifty 50 gained 159.50 points. Despite mixed Asian trends, foreign institutional buying supports the market amid easing geopolitical tensions.

items.title: U.S. loses to Belgium 4-1; Balogun plays after Trump calls FIFA
items.summary: FIFA reversed Folarin Balogun’s suspension after Trump sought a review, letting the U.S. striker play Belgium over objections.

items.title: Trump heads to Turkey as NATO is strained by Russian attacks, U.S. impatience
items.summary: Trump frequently vented about NATO members' refusal to heed U.S. calls for help clearing the Strait of Hormuz during its campaign against Iran.

items.title: Vaishali Parekh recommends three intraday stocks to buy or sell on Tuesday — 7 July 2026
items.summary: Buy or sell stocks, 7 July 2026: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — Lloyds Engineering Works, RBL Bank, and E I D-Parry (India).

items.title: Trump rings Wall Street opening bell, hails Trump accounts
items.summary: The tax-advantaged accounts are available to all minors who are US citizens, with the government providing a $1,000 seed contribution for children born between 2025 and 2028

items.summary: Stock market today: Gift Nifty was trading near the 24,578 mark, up over 95 points from the previous close of Nifty futures.

items.title: Inside GIFT City’s first physical gold fund: How investors can redeem actual gold bars
items.summary: The fund is restricted to non-resident investors, including institutions, family offices, and high-net-worth individuals, and is designed as a long-term allocation product with optional physical delivery

items.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 7 July
items.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a positive start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 24,584 level, a premium of nearly 102 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.

items.title: Asian stocks today: Nikkei, Kospi fall over 4% as technology stocks weigh; Samsung shares slide
items.summary: Asian stocks today: South Korea's Kospi fell 4%, while the small-cap Kosdaq index slipped 0.72%. Japan's Nikkei 225 traded marginally lower, although the broader Topix index edged up 0.60%.

items.title: Gift Nifty, Samsung earnings, bond yields to gold rate: 10 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
items.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,578 level, a premium of nearly 95 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a positive start for the Indian stock market indices.

items.title: Crude oil prices rise on hopes of stronger demand; Brent above $72 per barrel
items.summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures rose 28 cents, or 0.39%, to $72.29 a barrel, while US West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude climbed 29 cents, or 0.26%, to $68.84 a barrel.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Dow hits new high and football gets political
items.summary: U.S. markets return in a celebratory mood, with the Dow crossing 53,000 and the Nasdaq rising amid a chip rebound.

items.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today - 7 July 2026
items.summary: The Indian stock market ended Tuesday's session on a higher note. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.

items.title: Apple veteran's Chinese smart-glasses firm becomes unicorn as Tencent, Meituan fund rival to Meta
items.summary: The Shenzhen-based startup joined a slew of global peers to build advanced gadgets and electronics that bring the benefits of AI to individual users.

items.title: Fall in crude prices not enough to lift Rupee: Mint poll
items.summary: Economists predict the Indian rupee will remain stable at 94-96 against the US dollar despite falling crude oil prices. Unlikely to appreciate significantly, the rupee is expected to gradually weaken to around 96.5 by March 2027, as central bank policies absorb dollar inflows.

items.title: In a flurry of deals, Adani mobilizes $10 bn in a week for capex boost
items.summary: The fund mobilization spans equity raises, stake sales and long-term commitments from strategic partners, including global investors and sovereign-backed entities. The flurry of deals marks one of the largest capital-raising exercises by an Indian conglomerate in recent years.

items.title: Skyroot Aerospace: building the Uber for space, one launch at a time
items.summary: Skyroot is preparing for Vikram 1, a mission that will make it the first private Indian company to launch a commercial satellite. Co-founded by former Isro scientists Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka, the startup aims to capture a significant portion of the global launch market.

items.title: Beyond the rain hit, El Nino set to sap India's wind power output
items.summary: The El Nino weather pattern may do more than disrupt India's monsoon this year. It could also weaken wind speeds during the peak generation season, hurting India’s wind power output at a time of record electricity demand.

items.title: Robots available for rent: But what can they do?
items.summary: Robotics tech is changing fast, so for many it makes sense to rent a robot.

items.title: Amazon bars breastfeeding boss from business course
items.summary: Rachel Bews says she told on the way her child would not be allowed on site. Amazon has apologised for not communicating its policy clearly.

items.title: 'I wear it on my middle finger': The rise of the defiant divorce ring
items.summary: Around the world women are marking their split with new - or repurposed engagement - rings.

items.title: Auto retail sales surge 22% to record 2.56 million in June; Dealers expectgrowth momentumto continue withrains picking up
items.summary: Vehicle retail sales reached a record high in June, showing a significant year-on-year increase. This robust growth was supported by strong demand across various vehicle segments. Improved supplies and easing crude oil prices are expected to sustain this positive momentum. Two-wheeler sales saw a notable rise, with electric models gaining significant market share. Rural markets demonstrated strong traction in commercial vehicle sales, indicating broad economic recovery.

items.title: Banks accused of failing most vulnerable customers
items.summary: Homeless people or those in financial hardship have been pushed to online applications and away from basic bank accounts.

items.title: Phone contract comparisons 'amounted to mis-selling' student loans, MPs say
items.summary: A new report says students were not well-enough informed that their loan terms could change retrospectively.

items.title: Deposits win for private banks, loans trophy for PSBs: Q1 throws up opposing trends; private growth seen as healthier
items.summary: Private banks saw stronger deposit growth than public sector lenders in the June quarter. Public sector banks, however, achieved faster credit expansion during the same period. This divergence raises concerns about funding sustainability for state-run institutions. Private banks have steadily gained market share in deposits over a decade. Credit growth between the two bank types showed convergence in the recent quarter.

items.title: Can China repeat its EV success with robotaxis?
items.summary: China's self-driving car firms have been given a headstart by the country's EV supply chain as they expand globally.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, as Xbox unit downsizes and plans to spin off four gaming studios
items.summary: Microsoft is cutting jobs in its commercial business and its Xbox gaming group, where revenue has been shrinking.

items.title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation
items.summary: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba has put Anthropic's Claude Code on a high-risk software list.

items.title: Toyota to invest $3.6 billion to move Tacoma pickup truck production from Mexico to Texas
items.summary: Toyota said it is investing $3.6 billion to move production of the Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio manufacturing campus in Texas.

items.title: ET Graphics: Crude oil retreats, but India's fuel bills stay up
items.summary: China has reduced fuel prices, mirroring pre-war levels as crude oil costs decline. Indian private retailer Nayara Energy also cut its fuel rates recently. State-run firms have not yet followed suit after substantial first-quarter losses. These companies incurred nearly seventy-five thousand crore rupees in losses. This situation arose due to delayed fuel price adjustments by the government.

items.title: Auto companies may soon get to trade BEE credits for easier fuel-efficiency compliance
items.summary: The power ministry has introduced a new credit trading system aimed at Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency standards. Under this initiative, manufacturers can trade excess compliance credits and purchase them from the Bureau of Energy Efficiency. The framework includes credit and debit accounting, allowing manufacturers who surpass targets to earn credits, while those who don't accumulate debits.

items.title: NTPC gets into action to develop mega PWR-based nuclear projects
items.summary: NTPC plans to develop nuclear power capacity using pressurised water reactor technology. Global consultants are invited to prepare tender documents and evaluate bids. This initiative follows the promulgation of the SHANTI Act, allowing private sector participation. NTPC aims to build thirty gigawatts of nuclear capacity on its own. The company is also exploring tie-ups with Rosatom and EDF for PWR projects.

items.title: Sony Pictures Networks India profit climbs 16% in FY26 as sports drive growth
items.summary: For the fiscal year 2026, Sony Pictures Networks India recorded an impressive 16% surge in profit, largely thanks to a robust cricket schedule and rising advertising revenues. However, subscription income fell by 4% amid distribution disputes. In contrast, licensing income flourished after JioHotstar acquired digital rights. Total costs escalated by 8%, reflecting higher employee and marketing expenses.

items.title: Bharat Petroleum to expand retail offerings, EV charging network
items.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd is investing significantly to expand its retail offerings. The company aims to increase its market share by 2030. BPCL is expanding its electric vehicle charging network across major highways. It is also scaling up its Drive Fresh and Be Cafe initiatives. This strategy seeks to make travel more convenient for customers.

items.title: Regulator seeks views to curb similar sounding drug brands
items.summary: The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation in India is inviting public contributions concerning new regulations aimed at curbing the sale of various medications under similar brand names, which can mislead both patients and pharmacists about their contents. As part of this initiative, the drug regulator has released a notice for stakeholder observations, with a deadline for comments set for July 17 to ensure improved safety and trust among patients.

items.title: Private banks write off nearly half of NPAs in FY26, outpace PSBs
items.summary: "Private banks utilise these write-offs and provisioning buffers to proactively clean up their balance sheets, while public sector banks generally rely more heavily on aggregate long-term or systemic recovery," said Kuntal Sur, partner and leader for risk consulting, financial services and treasury, at PwC India. "Unsecured retail loans in general have shown deteriorations leading to higher write-offs."

items.title: MCX widens domestic refiner pool, revises delivery norms
items.summary: MCX has updated its norms to include BIS-certified bullion and more local refiners. This move aims to increase domestic gold and silver supplies significantly. It will particularly benefit silver, following recent import curbs and duty hikes. Approved refiners can now process recycled gold directly from the market. This creates a new ecosystem for monetizing household gold holdings.

items.title: Cooktops in high demand as West Asia war alters household habits
items.summary: Home appliance makers are boosting induction cooktop production significantly. This surge is driven by consumers adopting hybrid cooking methods after LPG disruptions. The market is expected to grow substantially by 2026. New brands are entering the segment, attracting significant consumer interest. Manufacturers are experiencing doubled orders compared to last year.

items.title: Kotak AMC, executives seek settlement in Essel Group debt dispute
items.summary: The case dates back to the 2019 Essel liquidity crisis, when a sharp fall in Zee Entertainment's share price triggered margin calls on pledged shares securing debt held by six Kotak fixed maturity plans (FMPs). The case relates to Kotak Mutual Fund's six close-ended debt schemes (FMPs), which had invested in zero coupon non-convertible debentures (NCDs) issued by Konti Infrapower & Multiventures and Edison Utility Works, part of the Essel Group, the promoter group of Zee Entertainment Enterprises.

items.title: To ride the EV wave, M&M takes premium path, Tata Motors bets on scale
items.summary: Mahindra and Tata Motors are adopting distinct electric vehicle strategies for India's growing market. Mahindra focuses on premium e-SUVs and profitability, planning six new models by FY31. Tata Motors targets broader scale with a wider product range and powertrain options. Tata aims for EVs to be thirty percent of its passenger vehicle sales. Analysts are divided on which company's approach will yield greater shareholder value.

items.title: Education Ministry in discussions to ease 1-km norm for EWS admissions
items.summary: The education ministry is urging states to update Right to Education Act rules. This action follows Supreme Court orders for better implementation of the EWS quota. High-level discussions are also underway for neighborhood school criteria flexibility. States are being asked to frame RTE rules as per the Supreme Court's directives.

items.title: Mission Re-search: Post-doctoral students to be retained in India
items.summary: The Indian government is developing a plan to retain talented researchers. This initiative aims to offer stronger financial and academic incentives for postdoctoral scholars. The number of research positions will significantly increase over the next decade. Stipends will be enhanced to match international standards, attracting global talent. This strategy seeks to strengthen India's research and development ecosystem.

items.title: India, EU aim to complete FTA legal scrubbing in 15-20 days, says Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Goyal will visit Spain, Belgium and Finland on July 14-15 with a delegation of business leaders as part of preparations for implementing the trade pact. He is also scheduled to meet EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic in Brussels. "We have already started marketing Indian goods and services so that we are future-ready to get the best out of the FTAs that we have finalised," Goyal said at an event organised by the Council for Leather Exports.

items.title: Govt plans LNG buffer mandate for terminals
items.summary: India is weighing a plan to fund emergency liquefied natural gas stockpiles. This initiative aims to bolster supply security after recent global disruptions. Policymakers are considering raising regasification tolls to finance expanded terminal storage capacity. Terminal operators could recover investment costs by charging higher fees to importers. This approach seeks to build storage faster than direct government financing.

items.title: Kharif sowing picks up, but still 21% below last year
items.summary: Kharif sowing lags significantly behind last year due to delayed and uneven monsoon rains. Paddy, pulses, and oilseeds acreage shows substantial year-on-year declines. This reduced planting raises concerns about potential crop output and food inflation. Sugarcane acreage has seen a slight increase, bucking the overall trend. Below-normal rainfall forecasts for July further exacerbate these agricultural challenges.

items.title: South-based developers step up Mumbai play amid strong housing demand
items.summary: Several large South Indian real estate developers are now entering Mumbai's property market. They are strategically focusing on redevelopment projects and joint development agreements. This approach offers a capital-efficient entry into India's most expensive housing market. Mumbai's sustained housing demand and redevelopment opportunities attract these new entrants. The trend is intensifying competition in the city's premium and mid-income housing segments.

items.title: Delhi buses may run on garbage-produced hydrogen in future: Nitin Gadkari
items.summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari envisions Delhi buses running on hydrogen fuel. This hydrogen will be produced from segregated municipal waste through biodigesters. He also revealed plans to eliminate all garbage nationwide by 2027. Eight million tons of Delhi's landfill waste have already aided expressway construction.

items.title: Mahindra bets on execution as global uncertainty becomes 'structural'
items.summary: Mahindra Group views global uncertainty as a permanent business feature. The conglomerate will focus on faster execution and calibrated investments. This strategy aims to capitalize on volatility rather than waiting for stability. The group reported its strongest financial performance in FY26. Future investments will target new technologies and manufacturing expansion.

items.title: Hedge fund run by ex-OpenAI researcher bets on SK Hynix’s US IPO
items.summary: Situational Awareness joins UK investor Baillie Gifford backing the South Korean memory maker’s American debut

items.title: NATO tensions are ‘growing pains,’ U.S. ambassador says as Trump presses allies
items.summary: U.S. Ambassador to NATO Matthew Whitaker said NATO's allies boosting defense spending under pressure from Trump reflects “growing pains,” not a crisis.

items.title: No breach of member, student or examination data, says ICAI
items.summary: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) on Monday stated that there has been no breach of records of its members, students or examination data. The apex body dismissed the claims as 'completely false, baseless and malicious.' It urged its stakeholders to not give credence to the alleged misinformation and asserted that it has robust systems in place to protect its data.

items.title: India, EU working to finish legal scrubbing of FTA text in 15-20 days: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and the European Union expect to finish legal scrubbing of their free trade agreement text soon. Both sides aim to sign the comprehensive trade pact by the end of this year. Implementation of this significant agreement is anticipated to occur next year. This pact will open twenty-seven developed countries for Indian businesses and exports. The leather industry is encouraged to target fifteen billion dollars in exports.

items.title: Microsoft Layoffs: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma acknowledges 'business is not healthy', flags weak margins amid 3200 job cuts
items.summary: Announcing the overhaul in a message to employees on Monday, Xbox Chief Executive Officer Asha Sharma acknowledged the challenges facing the division. She said the restructuring is aimed at simplifying Xbox's operations and redirecting resources towards larger projects.

items.title: Landing, parking charges waiver at major airports for airlines unlikely to continue
items.summary: The Airports Economic Regulatory Authority is unlikely to extend the waiver. This waiver reduced landing and parking charges for domestic flights by twenty-five percent. The three-month reduction was implemented in April due to airline financial challenges. Airport operators have not received any extension orders for the reduced charges. The waiver has now lapsed as no new directive has been issued.

items.title: Nvidia's next-gen AI rack system delayed to 2028 on manufacturing snags, SemiAnalysis says
items.summary: The reported delay adds to concerns that Nvidia's breakneck annual release cadence is colliding with manufacturing limits.

items.title: India's growth story 'remains intact', bets on rising consumer aspirations for future growth: Dabur
items.summary: Dabur India sees its growth story remaining intact despite global uncertainties. Rising consumer aspirations and strong domestic demand fuel the company's future expansion plans. The FMCG major reported a five percent revenue growth and profit increase for the fiscal. Dabur is investing in new facilities and premium offerings to attract younger consumers.

items.title: China steps up oil purchases from Middle East as prices fall
items.summary: Move comes as Saudi Arabia cuts pricing on exports to Asia to 6-year low

items.title: Yotta raises $150 million at $3.9 billion valuation to fund AI infrastructure expansion
items.summary: Yotta Data Services secured $150 million from investors for business expansion. The company plans to significantly increase its AI cloud capacity soon. This expansion aims to position Yotta among global AI compute leaders. They are also enhancing sovereign cloud and managed services offerings. Yotta seeks to establish India as an AI infrastructure producer.

items.title: Student loan servicers begin 90-day countdown for borrowers to leave SAVE plan
items.summary: Student loan servicers have begun alerting borrowers that they have 90 days to leave the Biden-era SAVE plan. Here's what comes next.

items.title: Trump defends call urging Balogun red card review: 'It wasn't a foul'
items.summary: "I didn't know what the hell a red card was," said Trump, who called FIFA President Gianni Infantino about the suspension of Folarin Balogun.

items.title: Essar Energy Transition ties up with Petraco for crude oil supply
items.summary: The facility enables Essar Energy Transition Fuels to diversify the crude sourcing and marketing options for its products

items.title: ICICI Prudential board approves reclassifying Prudential as an investor
items.summary: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance's board approved seeking Irdai's nod to reclassify Prudential Corp. Holdings and rename the company to ICICI Life Insurance Ltd.

items.title: PE, M&A deal values surge despite global conflicts on big-ticket cross-border transactions
items.summary: Overall deal value jumps 127% in Q2 to $36.3 billion even as volumes decline

items.title: Flipkart launches second ESOP liquidity event, lets employees encash up to 5% of vested stock
items.summary: The Walmart-owned e-commerce firm has fixed the liquidity price at ₹713.4 per option, with payouts scheduled for August

items.title: SEBI clears IPOs of Manipal Health, RentoMojo
items.summary: Temasek and the Dr Ranjan Pai-backed multispecialty hospitals chain Manipal Health Enterprises plans to raise ₹8,000 crore via fresh share, while existing shareholders proposed to sell 4.32 crore shares through OFS

items.title: Laser Power & Infra fixes price band to raise ₹3,000 crore via IPO
items.summary: The issue to open on Thursday, price band fixed at ₹203-₹214

items.title: Zinc mine output down in 2025, but that of lead was up
items.summary: However, utilisation of both metals increased in contrast to the 2024 trend

items.title: Millennium Towers: How Phoenix Mills is designing workplaces people choose
items.summary: Millennium Towers in Pune offers a new vision for commercial office spaces. This development integrates business, lifestyle, and community to support modern work needs. The campus provides amenities like landscaped podiums and a fitness club for employee well-being. Co-located Phoenix Mall of the Millennium offers extensive retail and entertainment options. This project emphasizes flexible design and resilient infrastructure for businesses.

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs and shrinks Xbox in 'significant restructure'
items.summary: The sweeping layoffs equate to 2.1% of Microsoft's workforce, with 1,600 immediate job losses at Xbox.

items.title: SBI MF set to enter private credit, unlisted equity via AIF
items.summary: RBI greenlight paves the way for the fund house to tap India’s $25-billion private credit market

items.title: Gold loans overtake vehicle loans as top securitised asset class in Q1: Crisil
items.summary: Gold loans accounted for 31% of securitisation volumes, driven by strong NBFC portfolio growth and healthy investor demand

items.title: Policy, energy, climate, geopolitics to influence global veg oil sector: IVPA
items.summary: They are increasingly influencing production, trade flows and price formation, says Sudhakar Desai, the association president

items.title: Citi to join ‘club’ of clearing banks controlling London gold market
items.summary: Bank to join JPMorgan, UBS, ICBC and HSBC as first new clearing member for world’s largest physical gold market in a decade

items.title: Lockheed Martin snaps up Ultra Maritime in $3.45 billion naval defence push: All about the deal
items.summary: The deal comes at a time when defence companies are benefiting from a surge in military spending worldwide.

items.title: ITV hits such as I'm a Celebrity to stay free to watch after Sky takeover
items.summary: Sky boss Dana Strong's comments came as the channel announces it is buying ITV's media and entertainment divisions in a £1.6bn deal.

items.title: LIC chief sees protection plans driving growth
items.summary: In the CEO message, R. Doraiswamy notes a shift towards term- and protection-oriented solutions and flags the pressure market volatility has placed on the insurer's participating funds.

items.title: NCR market will be as big or bigger than Mumbai for Oberoi Realty: Vikas Oberoi
items.summary: The Mumbai-based luxury developer's maiden project in Gurugram drew a staggering ₹30,000 crore order book, setting the stage for it to challenge established regional players in the booming northern market.

items.title: EasyJet reaches outline agreement on £5.5bn takeover by Castlelake
items.summary: UK airline’s board says it is minded to recommend proposal by US private credit group

items.title: OPEC+ to prioritise oil supply over price floor until Hormuz normalises; Brent likely to stay below $85: Analyst
items.summary: Kotak Securities expects the rupee to appreciate to 92-93 against the US dollar by September, with RBI intervention likely to curb further gains

items.title: Welcome to the age of the Profit Dollar
items.summary: The greenback is no longer a reserve currency — it has become a vehicle for unfettered capital accumulation

items.title: Broker’s Call: Vinati Organics (Accumulate)
items.summary: CD Research

items.title: Microsoft Layoffs: Xbox cuts 4,800 jobs and plans to spin off five gaming studios
items.summary: In all, planned cuts across Microsoft announced Monday, 6 July, total 6,400. That amounts to less than 3% of total headcount of about 228,000.

items.title: Saudi Arabia cuts oil prices for Asia customers by $11 a barrel in biggest reduction in 26 years
items.summary: Saudi Arabia cut the price of its main crude grade for customers in Asia in August by the most in at least 26 years, as a surge of global supply heightens competition for buyers.

items.title: The mental health crisis tea garden executives have long faced, now a study explores it
items.summary: A groundbreaking doctoral study by Dr. Pransu Raj Kaushik, awarded by Dibrugarh University, has illuminated the critical yet overlooked issue of workplace stress and mental health among tea garden executives. The research, a comparative analysis involving NETA member companies and BCPL, offers vital insights for improving employee well-being and productivity within the tea sector.

items.title: Prayagraj records over 20% unemployment rate among 46 surveyed cities with 10 lakh+ population: Report
items.summary: Prayagraj and Patna recorded over 20% unemployment rates among 46 major cities, while Ahmedabad and Kolkata had the lowest at 2.5%. The average unemployment rate stands at 6.8%.

items.title: 'Electronic warfare is a tech phenomenon': Why the market is rethinking defense valuations
items.summary: There's a growing investment in newer areas of defense such as deep strike capabilities, anti-drone and unmanned systems, with different priorities for different countries.

items.title: India office market defies global turmoil with GCC-led leasing
items.summary: The office market logged record H1 2026 leasing of 45.5 million sq ft, led by global capability centres and flex space operators even as geopolitical tensions weighed on sentiment. GCCs alone accounted for 43% of demand, with CBRE expecting them to drive over 40% of full-year absorption.

items.title: Lockheed Martin to buy naval defense firm Ultra Maritime for $3.45 billion
items.summary: Ultra is owned by private equity firm Advent International, and specialises in anti-submarine technology.

items.title: Versant agrees to buy golf simulator company Full Swing for $530 million
items.summary: The deal for Full Swing will expand Versant's nontraditional media assets in an effort to diversify revenue from cable television.

items.title: Govt relaxes verification norms for high capacity weighing instruments
items.summary: The government has significantly eased rules for verifying large weighing instruments, those with a capacity of one tonne or more. This move aims to boost business ease by reducing the need for heavy standard weights during checks. The revised regulations now require only 20% of the instrument's maximum capacity in standard weights, down from 50%, streamlining operations and cutting costs for industries and weighbridge owners.

items.title: Taking on HUL, P&G and Marico: Why Honasa wants to be more than just Mamaearth
items.summary: A few years ago, Mamaearth rewrote the beauty playbook. Now, with shifting consumer tastes and intense competition, the brand’s parent, Honasa Consumer, is looking to reinvent itself with a broader portfolio. How that plays out will determine if it's a one-brand wonder or grows as something bigger.

items.title: The supertanker tycoon making millions on Hormuz shuttle runs
items.summary: Sinokor Group’s ships traveled ‘dark’ without their transponders (and often under the cover of literal darkness) before offloading their cargo into other tankers waiting outside the waterway, and then returning back to collect more

items.title: Wegovy weight loss pill now available in UK - here's what you need to know
items.summary: The once-a-day pill, from the makers of the Wegovy weight-loss jab, can now be bought privately in UK pharmacies.

items.title: Delhi High Court orders Chinese smartphone maker Transsion to furnish security in InterDigital patent case
items.summary: Shenzhen opposed the request for a security deposit, arguing that InterDigital had failed to establish, even on a prima facie basis, the validity and essentiality of the patents in question

items.title: Trump's calls, Ukraine's strikes and Russia's barrage on Kyiv put markets on alert
items.summary: Trump reportedly spoke with Putin and Zelenskyy as Ukraine struck Russian targets and Moscow launched another deadly attack on Kyiv.

items.title: EasyJet shares soar 10% as budget airline agrees to $7.3 billion Castlelake takeover
items.summary: EasyJet shares surged in early dealmaking Monday after it agreed in principle to takeover bid from Castlelake.

items.title: Blinkit takes the premium groceries route with ‘Gourmet’ to lift order values
items.summary: Blinkit’s premium pilot operates through dedicated dark stores featuring niche brands, signalling the quick-commerce leader’s latest push to capture affluent shoppers and strengthen its competitive edge.

items.title: TPG, GIC and ICICI Bank acquire Aseem Infrastructure Finance for ₹5,000 crore
items.summary: Aseem is a leading sustainable infrastructure debt financier with a core focus on renewable energy, power transmission and other infrastructure assets in India. It was established in 2020 by the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund, India’s sovereign-anchored alternative asset manager.

items.title: Who is Maya Tata? Noel Tata's daughter to drive Westside’s e-commerce push
items.summary: Maya Tata has actively transitioned across different businesses within the Tata conglomerate. She began her career in finance at Tata Capital before moving to Tata Digital

items.title: EasyJet agrees 'in principle' to £5.2bn takeover deal
items.summary: The low-cost airline had previously rejected four takeover offers from US investment firm Castlelake.

items.title: CVC sells marina business for more than €1bn as yacht market booms
items.summary: French private equity firm InfraVia’s purchase of D-Marin is latest in a flurry of investments in sector

items.title: South Korean prosecutors indict four major refiners over allegations of oil price collusion
items.summary: South Korean prosecutors on Monday indicted four major domestic oil refiners for violating fair trade laws.

items.title: Suzuki Motor arm Next Bharat Ventures' ₹2,000 crore second impact fund to back rural entrepreneurs
items.summary: Next Bharat Venture’s second fund will focus on agriculture, financial inclusion, healthcare, and AI for social good among others.

items.title: Why Iran may find it difficult to clear its oil inventories even after sanctions relief
items.summary: Iran may face challenges in clearing oil inventories even after restrictions have been lifted, amid oil supplies elsewhere while China becomes less enthusiastic

items.title: VB-G RAM G: A potential rural income hike comes with a burden
items.summary: India's new rural employment scheme, VB-G RAM G, promises more work and higher wages, aiming to boost rural incomes. However, a significant shift in funding responsibility to states raises concerns. Many states could face a four to five-fold increase in their financial contribution, potentially forcing difficult budgetary trade-offs and impacting the scheme's overall effectiveness in reviving the rural economy.

items.title: What Sky buying ITV could mean for your favourite shows
items.summary: Sky is set to buy ITV's TV and streaming channels for up to £1.6bn, the companies announced on Monday.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Trump sees red, prediction markets score and NATO's defensive
items.summary: The World Cup takes an extraordinary turn after President Trump's reported red card intervention.

items.title: True North cashes out $2 billion for investors as PE firms step up exits
items.summary: This comes on the back of exits for True North from portfolio firms like FedFina, Niva Bupa, Biocon Biologics, Infinity Fincorp Solutions, Zydus Wellness and Home First Finance.

items.title: Epigamia to expand production capacity as value-added dairy market heats up
items.summary: Epigamia is expanding manufacturing capacity as its new CEO and COO settle in following 50% growth over two years, with Greek yogurt still generating more than half of company's revenue.

items.title: SaaSpocalypse deferred
items.summary: The moats hold, for now

items.title: Meta’s woes deepen in India as child abuse ads on Instagram draw government ire
items.summary: U.S. social media giant Meta is facing an onslaught of regulatory scrutiny in India, which has the largest user base for Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp.

items.title: How the Iran crisis undermines the Bank’s QT programme
items.summary: Unlike the previous crises, this time the Bank carried on selling. It is time for a rethink.

items.title: 'NATO 3.0': Defense spending pledges face the Trump test
items.summary: Leaders are expected to focus on whether Europe can turn higher defense spending into military power as Washington pushes allies to shoulder more of the burden.

items.title: Ebola outbreak containment hampered by USAID closure, experts say
items.summary: Foreign aid cuts have "demonstrably worsened" the Ebola crisis, a virologist told CNBC.

items.title: Why OpenAI and Anthropic may struggle to float
items.summary: The costs of remaining at the frontier of AI are punishing, but the penalties for falling behind may be even worse

items.title: Baillie Gifford pursues ‘voluntary exits’ under new strategy
items.summary: Scheme for UK-based staff comes as firm refocuses on areas such as family offices and financial advisers in US and Asia

items.title: UK ministers changed stance on biofuels after officials’ US lobbying tour
items.summary: Revelation raises concerns that US commercial pressure could weaken Britain’s efforts to reduce carbon emissions

items.title: Big investors commit billions to private credit despite turmoil
items.summary: Institutional investors poured money into funds as retail money fled

items.title: US regulator’s change of tack comes with risk of obscuring audit flaws
items.summary: The PCAOB has signalled a shift from individual checks towards firm-wide quality controls

items.title: Investors must be wary of the earnings bubble
items.summary: The real difference between today’s market boom and the late 1990s lies in capital expenditure

items.title: Trump’s war means higher global interest rates for years to come
items.summary: Global interest rates are set to remain higher for longer, with Bloomberg Economics forecasting borrowing costs elevated by up to half a percentage point through 2028. This shift, influenced by lingering inflation risks from the Iran conflict's energy shock and the AI race, means consumers and businesses face more expensive loans. Central banks, wary of past inflation surges, are maintaining hawkish stances, impacting economies worldwide.

items.title: High jewellery boosts desirability to outshine luxury sector
items.summary: Asset-rich buyers, soaring gold prices and big launches have raised perceptions of the category’s investment potential

items.title: Backlash after China bubble tea firm ordered to pay Louis Vuitton $1.5m
items.summary: A court in China ruled that Molly Tea had infringed on the luxury brand's four-petal flower design.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Red cards, record bets and market's risk-on mood
items.summary: Drama is not only on the pitch — it is showing up across prediction markets, oil and Wall Street's risk appetite.

items.title: Lenders temper Vi business plan, seek group's guarantor for massive loan
items.summary: Lenders are prevailing upon the company to scale back its financial projections, while also demanding a corporate guarantee from another Aditya Birla Group firm, according to two people aware of the development.

items.title: Three things you can do to stop EU border checks at the airport costing you
items.summary: Queues are expected at airports this summer owing to EU's new digital border control system.

items.title: Regulators in ‘arms race’ as millions use AI for personal finance, FCA warns
items.summary: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions

items.title: India and Russia set $50 billion investment target by 2030
items.summary: India and Russia are targeting $50 billion in mutual investments by 2030, focusing on joint ventures in advanced manufacturing, critical minerals, and green technology. A recent meeting in Moscow saw both nations agreeing to encourage private sector exploration and collaboration. India highlighted its cost-effective skilled workforce and investment-friendly reforms, while the "Making in Russia for India" model was also promoted to boost bilateral industrial ties.

items.title: Concerns rise over low procurement for onion buffer stock
items.summary: Government efforts to build a buffer stock of onions are lagging significantly, with less than 5% of the target procured despite multiple price hikes. This slow pace raises concerns about market stability and potential hoarding, especially with the upcoming festive season and weather uncertainties impacting the next crop. Farmers are reportedly selling at higher market rates, bypassing government procurement.

items.title: Fall in global crude prices brightens fiscal outlook, El Nino risks linger
items.summary: Easing West Asian tensions have significantly improved India's fiscal outlook, with economists now predicting a smaller deficit than previously feared. Lower global crude oil prices are reducing the government's subsidy burden and boosting revenue prospects. While subsidy pressures and potential agricultural impacts remain concerns, the improved energy price scenario offers a more favorable fiscal path for the nation.

items.title: 'America's still at the top': Why Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas says US is best place to build a startup
items.summary: Perplexity CEO and co-founder Aravind Srinivas says he always thought America's the only country where a person can have an idea, and people will listen to it and encourage to pursue it

items.title: Europe’s richest man Bernard Arnault told to pay nearly ₹245 crore in taxes – How much is net worth of LVMH founder?
items.summary: French court orders LVMH CEO Bernard Arnault and his wife to pay nearly ₹245 crore in taxes, social contributions and wealth tax.

items.title: Nykaa expects Q1 revenue growth near 30% on fashion rebound
items.summary: FSN E-Commerce Ventures expects GMV growth near 30-33%, led by a fashion surge aided by its Nike partnership.

items.title: 'Start work at 11' - but will other bosses be as flexible over England's 1am match?
items.summary: Employers are being urged to use their "common sense" to allow staff to work flexibly where they can.

items.title: Tesla puts the brakes on AI costs, caps staff spending at ₹19,000 weekly, starting July 6
items.summary: Tesla is limiting staff spending on AI tools to ₹19,000 per week starting July 6, requiring manager approval for more, after some engineers reportedly spent thousands on AI tokens each week.

items.title: Govt extends deadline for WhatsApp's response over username issue to July 9: Report
items.summary: The mater pertains to security concerns over Meta-owned platform's recently announced usernames feature that will allow users to communicate without sharing their phone numbers.

items.title: Falling oil prices give RBI more room to focus on growth, but relief uneven across Asia: Standard Chartered
items.summary: Falling crude oil prices are providing a significant boost to India and other Asian nations, allowing central banks to prioritize economic growth. This "disinflationary relief" eases pressure on external accounts, especially for heavy energy importers like India, Thailand, and the Philippines. While some economies like South Korea may still face inflation due to AI booms, the overall trend favors easing.

items.title: Mint Explainer: How do sovereign guarantees work and why does India use them?
items.summary: Disclosed annually in the Union budget, these multi-crore commitments act as a crucial safety net for statutory corporations, though they carry potential default risks for the national exchequer.

items.title: ICICI Bank, Prudential sign undertaking to defuse conflict-of-interest concerns ahead of Bharti Life deal
items.summary: The undertaking, signed between the two promoters of ICICI Prudential Life on Saturday, seeks to address potential conflicts of interest arising from Prudential's proposed acquisition of Bharti Life.

items.title: Purabi Dairy, NDDB and Suzuki R&D India sign MoU to establish compressed biogas plant in Assam
items.summary: Assam is set to boost sustainable development with a new compressed biogas plant. The National Dairy Development Board, Suzuki R&D India, and North East Dairy and Foods Ltd. have partnered to convert agricultural and dairy waste into clean biogas and organic fertiliser. This initiative promises to enhance rural livelihoods, promote scientific waste management, and contribute to cleaner mobility, aligning with the state's green goals and fostering a circular economy.

items.title: India's outbound travel defies West Asia turmoil as Thailand, Australia, Singapore and US woo tourists
items.summary: Indian outbound tourism is showing no signs of slowing. Thailand has recorded 1.19 million Indian arrivals in 2026, while Singapore has received nearly half a million visitors.

items.title: Aluminium industry divided over removing custom duties for scraps and finished products
items.summary: India's aluminium industry is split over import duties. Primary producers, represented by the Aluminium Association of India (AAI), want stricter controls on aluminium scrap imports and quality regulations, arguing that rising imports of allegedly substandard scrap could threaten more than ₹3 lakh crore of planned investments.

items.title: Community apps find new ways to cash in on gated communities
items.summary: Firms like MyGate, Nobrokerhood and Park+ are using their access to affluent gated communities using divergent strategies to build sustainable businesses beyond subscription models.
